risk_notes: All three npm dependencies use caret (^) ranges rather than exact pins, so transitive minor/patch versions can shift between installs.
No lockfile (package-lock.json or npm-shrinkwrap.json) is referenced; reproducible builds are not guaranteed.
Connects to an unauthenticated external WebSocket endpoint 'wss://api.brokerage.com/live' with no certificate pinning or credential handling shown.
Silent fallback from real broker to mock data on WS error could mask integrity issues in production trading logic.
Dashboard WebSocket uses plain ws:// (not wss://) on the client side — no transport encryption between browser and server.
No authentication/authorization on the /api/trades endpoint or dashboard WebSocket; any local network client can read trade history and receive trade events.
better-sqlite3 is a native module requiring a C++ toolchain at install time, expanding the build-time supply-chain surface.

supply_chain_meaning: Static manifest/install-pattern scan; LLM dependency extraction from agent output; OSV lookup attempted (no matching advisories or no pinned versions).
